other_info:
Publications
A Comparison of SAT-based and SMT-based Frameworks for X-value Combinational Equivalence Checking

IEEE VLSI-SOC

Show publication
Abstract—X-value combinational equivalence checking (XCEC) is a critical problem in verifying refinement-based logic optimizations and ensuring the correctness of low power design methodologies. The critical challenge in XCEC is to establish equivalence in a dramatically expanded search space. We can modify the traditional SAT-based Boolean combinational equivalence checking (CEC) methods to handle X-valued logic by adding an extra bit. However, the XCEC problem becomes more challenging when the two models are X-value equivalent because it involves considering the entire search space to prove the equivalence. Therefore, as an alternative, we propose to use SMT-based frameworks for XCEC by devising appropriate transformations. The proposed formulation allows the SMT solver to pre-process and simplify the encoded satisfiability problem internally. As a result, the SMT-based framework for XCEC is more efficient than a pure SAT-based XCEC, especially for cases in which the revised model is a valid refinement of the given golden model. We have used Cryptominisat5 to implement SAT-based XCEC and STP (with Cryptominisat5 as an internal SAT solver) to implement SMT-based XCEC. Using ICCAD 2020 benchmark suite, we demonstrate that, on an average SMT-based approach is 2.5* faster than the SAT-based approach for X-value equivalent cases.

Autoguided Accident Avoiding Bumper Vehicle

International Journal for Research in Applied Science & Engineering Technology (IJRASET). 

In this paper we have introduced an innovative idea which could help us to reduce fatalities due to accidents. Our paper is based on the mechanism of Rack and pinion which is fitted inside the bumper of a car. This bumper can increase the time of impact and hence reduce the impulse or shock from an accident. This mechanism can be used along with the current system such as air bag and anti-breaking system. Also we have used GSM module to alert the person’s relatives and ambulance when any accident happen.

projects.raw_text:
X-Value Equivalence Checking (ICCAD 2020 | Cadence)

May 2021 – Present

Design of an Algorithm and Heuristics such as Partitioning to incorporate X-Values in the netlists.

STP is used as the SMT Solver and ICCAD 2020 Benchmark Suite is

used to verify the correctness of the Algorithm.

Skills: Formal Verification

Memory Compiler: Array Design & Replica Path Design

Sep 2021 – Dec 2021

Design of the Critical Path for a High-Speed Dual Port SRAM Memory Compiler.

Skills: Test Circuit

Design Test to identify WL coupling fault in Dual port SRAM

Jan 2021 – May 2021

Designed an ST-Dual Port SRAM and a Test circuit to detect Word Line Coupling Fault. Designed a 256*1K Memory Array and tested the WL Fault at the Worst Case.

Skills: Memory Test

RTL to GDS: Sequential Circuit having Counter and Comparator

Jan 2021 – May 2021

Designed a Sequential Circuit from RTL to GDS and performed Static Timing Analysis and DFT Analysis.

Skills: Chip design

AOI321: Schematic and Layout

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Designed AOO321 Complex Gate with Good PPA. Compared the Pre-Layout and Post-Layout Results.

Skills: Digital Designs

Gain Boosting in Fully Differential Folded Cascode Amplifier

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Designed Single Stage Fully differential Cascode Amplifiers with and without Gain Boosting and Compared their Results.

Skills: Analog Circuit Design

Library System

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Designed a Library System for an online Library using Python. Features: Issuing, Reserving, Returning of Books.

Sidebar based Accelerator Design

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

A Scratchpad based Sidebar was introduced which is shared directly between the Accelerator and the Host CPU.

Auto-Guided Accident Avoiding Smart Bumper Vehicle 

The project consists of a microcontroller controlled car with a smart bumper. The concept of the project was to reduce the casualties when accidents happen. Our project was designed in such a way that it can be implemented with the existing technology like air bag system and Anti Breaking System. The idea in our project is that when the vehicle senses any obstacle it changes its path and when accident happens it reduces the impulse of impact by using the bumper.  

Density Based Traffic Light System using Microcontroller 

The project consists of a model of efficient traffic light system. It consists of IR sensors which give information to a microcontroller and the microcontroller makes a decision based on the number of vehicles that passed through the sensors. Based on the decision the time of green light of a particular lane will be increased depending upon the number of vehicles.
